Hey what's going everyone? Welcome to Lead Nurture Principles, understanding these two pillars that sit and how successful you'll be with getting leads in the dorm. So let's get to it. So the lead nurture process is a combination of speed at gym launch in general still is speed is king. These are some of the best practices that we know. You want to make sure that you call immediately. You have your Zephyr SMS setup so that your phone should buzz the moment you have a new lead and you should immediately tap to call. And you should do that twice. So you should be doing a double dial immediately upon receiving every lead. Make sure that you always follow FTC guidelines at the time of this making it's 8am to 9pm in terms of call hours just to make sure that you don't cross any lines you're not supposed to. So anywho, Zephyr SMS tap to call that will decrease how much you work. So the faster you are the less you will have to work. And so if you can stay on it then you will have to call way less and spend less time calling and more time selling. The other piece of this is that the faster you contact the lead from the time they opt in, the speed increases the proximity to their pain and increases the proximity to their interest. So the further out you are the less aware of their pain they are, the less interested they are in the thing that they opted into. So if you opt in and I call you within 5 minutes you remember exactly what I'm talking about you can schedule you come in that afternoon. If you opt in and I don't really call you until day 2 tomorrow and I only call once from a number I don't know like the likelihood that you get me. But speed is king. Speed is king. You want to work last? Be faster. The second piece is volume is king and the reason I didn't say queen is because they are twins they are twin pillars to making this work and the reason I have workload realities is the second point here is that this will be the work. This is the work. This is the work of gym lunch. This is the part that will separate whether you will succeed or not. I'm just being very candid. The more reach outs you have the more people will schedule and show. Period. It is the number one predictor of how much money you will make is the number of reach outs you have. So if you want to decrease the amount of reach outs you have call faster but still the number of driver once we looked at all these campaigns is directly proportional with how many calls are being made. How many reach outs are being made. Just a quick you see the second main set of the points is volume, money, math. All is what we have figured out the ROI of a call is. So we have tracked calls over hundreds of campaigns when we were running these on our own and the 50 calls in an hour or you can take you all day. So $10 per call so if you feel like you are like man I don't feel like doing this just think about getting paid a $10 bill at your employee. So if you had set the goal to make $50,000 for a launch that would mean 100 sales. That would mean you should have roughly 400 leads that means you are closing 25% of your sales which is a great conversion number. If you are doing 25% you are doing awesome. So if you have 100 sales and you sell 500 that is $50,000 over 30 and following our $10 per call metric we know we have 5000 calls over 30 days which means your action item is 167 calls a day if you have a one month long campaign. That is what it boils down to. You can forget everything else that is here and just understand that you need to make 167 calls a day. So that means you can just call for hour for four hours roughly and you will hit it. So if you do that for a month so speed and volume those are the primary principles of the primary drive process. I mean it. Volume and speed.
